narrative: OMX30 opens to mixed signals: continental peers closed -0.30% to -0.41% while US finished modestly positive (S&P +0.38%, NASDAQ +0.62%), but Asian deterioration (Nikkei -2.79%, Shanghai -1.85%) has tempered risk appetite. The 4.8% premium to 200-MA suggests underlying strength, yet MACD trades below signal with a negative histogram (-9.818), RSI sits neutral at 45.19, and critically the stochastic K=0.1 is severely oversold—classic mean reversion territory. ADX at 20.5 with bearish DI bias and Bollinger %B at 0.05 (lower half) confirm we are at technical inflection: either OMX30 bounces or rolls over. The -87bp collapse in US 10Y yield (now 4.545%) is the wild card—devastating for bank NIM expansion and raising recession fears despite equity market superficial calm.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a critical technical cross at open. The index is +4.8% above its 200-day but trades 0.5% below the 50-MA (3180), with stochastic K bottomed at 0.1—textbook oversold but ADX strength only moderate (20.5, bearish DI skew). Watch for bounce towards 3145–3160 on short-covering, but conviction is low given continental weakness (-0.31% CAC) and the 87bp cliff in US yields crushing the refinancing story. Copper's 1.35% pop supports Boliden and Volvo B (industrial cyclicals), but oil flat (-0.22%) and gold down (-0.33%) limit mining upside. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) face headwinds as rate-cut expectations surge; expect rotation into defensives (AstraZeneca, Essity). Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—any softness amplifies risk-off. Resistance 3180 (50-MA), support 3085 (lower Bollinger), pivot 3123. Reduce position size pending clarity.
drivers: Continental Europe weakness (-0.3% DAX/CAC) outweighs US support
OMX30 stochastic deeply oversold (K=0.1) signals mean reversion risk but ADX only 20.5 limits conviction
Copper strength (+1.35%) supports industrials but oil flat and yields collapsing (-87bp) compress bank NIM outlook

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield collapsed 87bp to 4.545%; NIM compression fears now dominate. Curve flattening suggests market is pricing recession/rate cuts, headwind to Swedish bank valuations.
Copper +1.35% provides input cost relief and signals industrial demand resilience. USD/SEK soft (-0.62%) is slight negative for export pricing, but cyclical support from commodities bounce offsets.
Copper +1.35% is a direct revenue driver for Boliden despite gold down 0.33%. Copper strength outweighs gold weakness.
Yields plunging and broad risk-off bias favors defensive rotation. Expect modest inflow pressure as portfolio managers de-risk.
Yield collapse (4.545% vs prior 4.619%) cuts discount rates for property valuations. Refinancing concerns ease but cap-rate compression offsets in current sentiment.
NASDAQ +0.62% provides modest tailwind, but yield compression is a DCF headwind. Mixed signals net to neutral; wait for clarity.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.35% is a direct profit driver; gold weakness contained. Technical setup at 3123 pivot offers entry if broader index bounces.
Yield collapse (-87bp) is brutal for NIM thesis; market repricing lower-for-longer rates. Risk of dividend cut if earnings disappoint.
Risk-off sentiment and yield collapse favor defensive rotation. Pharma should outperform if equities weaken further.
watchList.catalyst: Risk sentiment, DCF resilience in lower-rate environment

watchList.reason: Mixed: copper strength supports industrial sentiment, but weak USD/SEK (-0.62%) headwind and yield cut fears temper cyclical enthusiasm. Wait for direction clarity.

keyLevels.resistance: 3180 (50-MA, neutral but now facing yield headwinds)

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—key risk event. Soft claims data would amplify rate-cut expectations and pressure equities. No major Swedish releases.
topRisk: US Jobless Claims miss (softer) could trigger flight-to-safety rally that crushes risk assets including OMX30 industrials and banks. Alternatively, hot inflation data could reverse yield collapse and trigger whipsaw.
technicalSummary: Stochastic K=0.1, D=15.4: OVERSOLD, mean reversion risk flagged
MACD below signal line, histogram -9.818: bearish setup, no buy signal yet
ADX 20.5 with bearish DI: moderate trend strength but directional bias DOWN—low confidence per rule
Bollinger %B=0.05 (lower half), width 3.98%: volatility elevated, support key
